steps.title: Set up the contrast
steps.content: The premise: P6 actually rated abstract and cubist equally. The example must capture what the model predicted for those two styles, contrasted against P6's equal ratings.

steps.title: Match the data
steps.content:
If the graph shows the model's predicted ratings for abstract and cubist were different for P6, then the model's predictions diverge from P6's actual equal ratings. That's (D): the model predicted P6's ratings would differ.

(A) and (C) pick a specific direction not supported by the assumption of equal actual ratings. (B) just restates the actual ratings.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
